metadata.title: Government Schemes [India & States] Current Affairs MCQs

questions.question: The INSPIRE Scheme is implemented by which department?

options.label: C
options.text: Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances

options.label: D
options.text: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade

questions.explanation: The INSPIRE Scheme (Innovation in Science Pursuit for Inspired Research), implemented by the Department of Science and Technology, aims to attract meritorious youth to pursue basic and natural sciences and strengthen India’s Research & Development base. The scheme has supported large numbers of students in recent years, with INSPIRE-MANAK beneficiaries around 50,000 annually, while INSPIRE-SHE supports nearly 9,000–10,000 students per year.

questions.question: What is the name of the scheme approved to support exporters amid West Asia logistics disruptions?

options.label: B
options.text: RELIEF (Resilience & Logistics Intervention for Export Facilitation)

answer.label: B
answer.text: RELIEF (Resilience & Logistics Intervention for Export Facilitation)

questions.explanation: Government approved RELIEF (Resilience & Logistics Intervention for Export Facilitation) under Export Promotion Mission with ₹497 crore outlay to support exporters amid West Asia disruptions. RELIEF provides enhanced risk coverage through Export Credit Guarantee Corporation, offering up to 100% cover for existing shipments (Feb 14–Mar 15, 2026). For upcoming consignments (Mar 16–Jun 15, 2026), exporters get government-supported coverage up to 95% risk. MSME exporters without ECGC cover receive up to 50% reimbursement of freight and insurance costs (max ₹50 lakh). The scheme is part of the Export Promotion Mission (2025–31) to boost export competitiveness.

questions.question: The Biotech-KISAN scheme is implemented by which department?

options.label: A
options.text: Department of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare

options.label: D
options.text: Department of Social Justice and Empowerment

questions.explanation: Department of Biotechnology implements Biotech-KISAN (Krishi Innovation Science Application Network) since 2017 to connect lab research with farmers. The scheme established more than 50 Biotech-KISAN hubs across 25 States/UTs, covering diverse agro-climatic regions. It has benefited over 1 lakh farmers with 15–37% yield improvement through demonstrations and training. It promotes sustainable practices like biofertilizers, precision farming, integrated pest management, and residue-free cultivation.

questions.question: Which is the nodal ministry for the Jiyo Parsi Scheme?

options.label: A
options.text: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment

options.label: B
options.text: Ministry of Women and Child Development

options.label: D
options.text: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare

questions.explanation: The Universal Parsi Registration Drive facilitation camp, organised by the Ministry of Minority Affairs, led to around 300 new registrations on the Jiyo Parsi Scheme portal. The Jiyo Parsi Scheme is a Central Sector Scheme launched in 2013–14 to address the declining population of the Parsi community in India. The scheme aims to stabilize and increase the Parsi population through scientific, medical, and social interventions. The Ministry of Minority Affairs serves as the nodal ministry for the scheme.

questions.question: The Production Linked Incentive Scheme for Food Processing Industry (PLISFPI) is implemented by which ministry?
questions.explanation: The Production Linked Incentive Scheme for Food Processing Industry has achieved cumulative investment of over ₹9,000 crore, exceeding the committed ₹7,000 crore, indicating strong industry response. It was launched by the Ministry of Food Processing Industries to boost India’s food processing sector. The scheme aims to promote Indian food brands globally and create large-scale food manufacturing champions. It focuses on increasing value addition, expanding processing capacity, and generating employment, especially in rural and off-farm sectors. The scheme is being implemented for six years from FY 2021–22 to FY 2026–27.

questions.question: RELIEF Scheme was launched by which ministry?

options.label: D
options.text: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare

questions.explanation: The Government has expanded the RELIEF (Resilience and Logistics Intervention for Export Facilitation) scheme under the Export Promotion Mission (EPM) due to geopolitical disruptions in West Asia affecting maritime trade. The RELIEF scheme, launched by Ministry of Commerce & Industry in March 2026. It provides support to Indian exporters facing high freight costs, increased insurance premiums, and war-related risks in the Gulf region. It is implemented through the Export Credit Guarantee Corporation of India as the nodal agency, focusing on insurance facilitation and risk coverage.

questions.question: PM Vishwakarma Scheme was launched by which ministry?

options.label: A
options.text: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises

options.label: C
options.text: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment

options.label: D
options.text: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology

answer.label: A
answer.text: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises

questions.explanation: The Ministry of Micro Small and Medium Enterprises trained over 2,500 artisans in Artificial Intelligence tools under the PM Vishwakarma Scheme. The initiative aims to integrate traditional craftsmanship with modern digital technology for livelihood enhancement. Around 2,543 artisans from multiple states participated, with highest participation from Telangana, Maharashtra, Gujarat, Rajasthan, and Bihar. PM Vishwakarma is a central sector scheme launched by Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises. It preserves heritage crafts while giving artisans access to modern tools, credit, and markets.

questions.question: Technology Development and Investment Promotion (TDIP) Scheme has been launched by which organization?
questions.explanation: The Union Minister of Communications and Development of North Eastern Region, Shri Jyotiraditya M. Scindia launched revised guidelines for the Technology Development and Investment Promotion (TDIP) Scheme. The Technology Development and Investment Promotion (TDIP) Scheme has been launched by the Department of Telecommunications (DoT) to boost global telecom standardization. It has an outlay of ₹203 crore for 2026–2031. It supports India’s participation in global bodies like International Telecommunication Union, 3GPP (Third Generation Partnership Project). It funds global meetings, technical contributions, and leadership roles.

questions.question: NAMASTE scheme is jointly implemented by which ministries?

options.label: A
options.text: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change and Ministry of Finance

options.label: B
options.text: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment and the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs

options.label: C
options.text: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare and Ministry of Law and Justice

options.label: D
options.text: Ministry of Finance and Ministry of Home Affairs

answer.label: B
answer.text: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment and the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs

questions.explanation: Recently, the Union Minister stated that the NAMASTE Scheme has shown measurable success in eliminating hazardous manual sanitation practices. NAMASTE (National Action for Mechanised Sanitation Ecosystem) aims to improve the safety, health, and dignity of sanitation workers. It is jointly implemented by the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment and the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs. The implementing agency is National Safai Karmacharis Finance Development Corporation. The scheme runs from FY 2023–24 to FY 2025–26.

questions.question: The Remission of Duties and Taxes on Exported Products (RoDTEP) Scheme is administered by which department?

options.label: C
options.text: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade

questions.explanation: The Department of Commerce issued revising schedules under the Remission of Duties and Taxes on Exported Products Scheme. The revision aligns RoDTEP tariff lines with updates in the Customs Tariff Act, 1975 via the Finance Act, 2026. The scheme was launched in 2021 under the amended Foreign Trade Policy 2015–20. It replaced the Merchandise Exports from India Scheme after it was found non-compliant with World Trade Organization rules. Its objective is to refund hidden taxes and duties not reimbursed by other mechanisms. It covers taxes incurred during manufacturing and distribution of exported goods. The scheme is administered by the Department of Revenue under the Ministry of Finance.
